errors: ; The ELM implementation does not include a check for the 10‑year ASCVD risk threshold of ≥10 % required to trigger the statin recommendation.; There is no logic to selectively offer statin therapy for patients with a 10‑year risk of 7.5 %–<10 % (Grade C).; The dyslipidemia risk factor is defined only by LDL > 130 mg/dL or HDL < 40 mg/dL; the CPG also requires elevated total cholesterol as a dyslipidemia indicator, which is missing.; The exclusion criterion “Patients with contraindications to statin therapy” is not represented in the ELM.

; The ELM implementation for **NumberConfirmedConditions** uses `ToConcept(C.verificationStatus) ~ Concept "Condition Confirmed"`. The CPG specifies a filter on `verificationStatus = "confirmed"` with the code system `http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-ver-status`. Unless the concept “Condition Confirmed” is explicitly defined to map to that exact code and system, the filter may not correctly enforce the required condition.; The **Summary** expression is represented only as `[Concatenate]` in the ELM snippet, with no explicit string format. The CPG requires the summary to be exactly `{PatientFirstName} has {N} confirmed conditions and {M} MedicationRequests.`. The ELM does not demonstrate that this format is produced.

The ELM implementation does not include a check for a qualifying encounter during the measurement period (value set “Encounter to Screen for Depression” – 2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916). This omission means the inclusion criterion requiring a qualifying encounter is not enforced.

; Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires minimum age ≥ 18 years, but ELM implements minimum age ≥ 25 years.; Encounter look‑back interval mismatch: CPG requires a 12‑month (1 year) look‑back for prior blood‑pressure screening encounters, but ELM uses a 6‑month interval.
The ELM implementation uses a HbA1c threshold of **≥ 9 g%**, whereas the CPG requires a threshold of **≥ 6.5 %**. This numeric value (and its unit) does not match the CPG requirement.

Rationale does not include the hrHPV recommendation; Source field missing; Has Recent Pap Smear does not verify that a result is present; Recommendation output does not include Links, Suggestions, or Errors for the false case.

The smoking history logic in the ELM implementation only checks for an active smoking status (`C.clinicalStatus.coding[0].code = active`). It does not account for former smokers, the required 20 pack‑year history, or the requirement that former smokers quit within the past 15 years. This does not fully implement the CPG’s inclusion criteria for smoking history.

; **Age threshold mismatch**: CPG requires patients aged **45–75** years, but the ELM implementation uses **60–75** years.; **Screening lookback period mismatch**: CPG specifies a lookback of **1 year** for a recent colorectal screening test, whereas the ELM implementation checks for a test within the past **2 years**.; **Recommendation text mismatch**: CPG requires the recommendation to explicitly list the modalities (“FOBT annually, FIT annually, stool DNA every 1‑3 years, colonoscopy every 10 years, or CT colonography every 5 years”), but the ELM recommendation is a generic placeholder (“Perform colorectal cancer screening using an appropriate modality (String)”).; **Missing Rationale**: CPG mandates a rationale string when screening is needed; the ELM implementation sets Rationale to null regardless of the population status.; **Missing Indicator**: CPG requires an indicator of “warning”; the ELM implementation does not provide any indicator.; **Missing Source**: CPG requires the source “USPSTF 2021 Colorectal Cancer Screening Recommendation”; the ELM implementation does not include this.

Time interval mismatch: CPG requires 1 year lookback, ELM uses 6 months; Missing Rationale: CPG requires rationale when screening needed, ELM returns null; Missing Indicator: CPG requires 'warning' indicator, ELM does not provide; Missing Source: CPG requires source 'USPSTF 2023 Anxiety Disorders Screening Recommendation', ELM does not provide

; **Age threshold mismatch**: The CPG requires patients aged **15–65** years, but the ELM implementation uses an age threshold of **≥30** years and does not enforce the upper bound of 65 years.; **Missing upper age bound**: The ELM logic does not check that the patient’s age is ≤65, violating the inclusion criteria.; **Incorrect demographic definition**: The ELM’s “In Demographic” definition only checks for age ≥30, whereas the CPG requires a range of 15–65.; **Missing rationale for recommendation**: The ELM sets the rationale to `null` for both cases, whereas the CPG specifies a detailed rationale when screening is recommended.; **Missing indicator and source**: The ELM implementation does not provide the required “indicator” (e.g., warning) or the “source” (USPSTF 2019 HIV Screening Recommendation) that the CPG mandates.

The ELM implementation incorrectly sets the lower age bound to 30 years in the **In Demographic** definition. The CPG requires patients to be aged **21 to 65** years, but the ELM uses **age ≥ 30** and **age ≤ 65**. This mismatch means the logic will exclude women aged 21–29 who should receive screening.

; The ELM implementation uses a 3‑month lookback period for recent depression screening (both adult and adolescent) whereas the CPG requires a 1‑year lookback period.; The ELM implementation does not include a check for a qualifying encounter during the measurement period (value set “Encounter to Screen for Depression”).

; **Age threshold mismatch**: The CPG requires patients to be **≥18 years** old, but the ELM implementation uses **≥25 years** in the `In Demographic` definition.; **Rationale content mismatch**: The CPG’s rationale includes mention of **pregnant women**, **brief behavioral counseling interventions**, and a **Grade B recommendation**. The ELM’s rationale omits these elements, providing only a partial statement.

; The ELM implementation checks female status with the literal string “female” ([Patient].gender.value = female) instead of using the required Female Administrative Sex value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.560.100.2).; The DEXA scan look‑back period is set to 6 months ([S.issued IN [Today - 6 months to Today]]) whereas the CPG requires a 2‑year look‑back period for a prior DEXA scan.

The ELM implementation uses an age lower bound of 55 years in the `In Demographic` definition, whereas the CPG requires a lower bound of 65 years. All other numeric values and logical structures match the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation defines **MeetsInclusionCriteria** as `([Screening Age] OR [Gender]) AND [Mammogram]`, which uses an OR between the age and gender checks. According to the CPG, both the age and gender criteria must be satisfied simultaneously (AND). This logical mismatch means the implementation may incorrectly include patients who are either 40+ years old or female, but not necessarily both.

The ELM implementation’s `InPopulation` logic does not incorporate the requirement that a recent tobacco use screening (within the past 1 year with a result present) must be absent. Specifically, `Needs Tobacco Use Screening` is defined only as `[In Demographic]` (age ≥ 18) and `InPopulation` is simply `[Needs Tobacco Use Screening]`. It should be `[In Demographic] AND NOT [Has Recent Tobacco Screening]` to correctly reflect the CPG’s inclusion criteria. This omission means the CDS may recommend screening even when a recent screening has already been performed.

; Age upper bound (≤ 80 years) is not enforced in the ELM implementation.; Smoking history criteria are incomplete: the ELM only checks for an active smoking status (`clinicalStatus.coding[0].code = active`) and does not account for former smokers who quit within the past 15 years.; The required 20 pack‑year smoking history is not evaluated.; The 15‑year quit window for former smokers is not implemented.

; **Connective Tissue Disorders logic inverted** – The ELM defines `Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)` as `NOT (EXISTS (ActiveCondition(Confirmed(...))))`, which is true when *no* confirmed active CTD exists. The CPG requires exclusion when a confirmed active CTD *does* exist, so the logic should be `EXISTS (ActiveCondition(Confirmed(...)))`.; **Inclusion condition too restrictive** – The ELM requires the IPF condition to be both *confirmed* and *active* (`ActiveCondition(Confirmed(...))`). The CPG only specifies that the patient has a diagnosis of IPF; it does not mandate confirmation or activity status.; **InPopulation logic incorrectly negates the exclusion criteria** – Because the CTD logic is inverted, `InPopulation` becomes `MeetsInclusionCriteria AND NOT (NOT CTD OR Amiodarone)`, which effectively requires the presence of a CTD and no amiodarone, the opposite of the intended rule.

; The ELM implementation does not include the **Bipolar Diagnosis** exclusion criterion; the `Needs Depression Screening` logic omits `NOT [Has Bipolar Diagnosis]`.; The ELM implementation does not check for a **qualifying encounter** during the measurement period (value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916`).; Consequently, the ELM logic does not fully implement the CPG’s `MeetsInclusionCriteria` which requires all four conditions (age, no bipolar, no depression, no recent screening) and the encounter requirement.

The ELM implementation incorrectly requires a colonoscopy in the past 1 month for inclusion.; **Colonoscopy definition**: `COUNT(...) > 0` (true when a colonoscopy exists) but the CPG requires *no* colonoscopy in the past month for screening to be needed.; **MeetsInclusionCriteria**: uses `[Required Age] AND [Colonoscopy]`, which selects patients who have had a colonoscopy, whereas the CPG logic should be `[Required Age] AND NOT [Colonoscopy]`.; Consequently, the recommendation is incorrectly triggered for patients who already had a colonoscopy.

The ELM implementation incorrectly requires a recent falls risk assessment to trigger screening.; **Needs Falls Prevention Screening** is defined as `[In Demographic] AND [Has Recent Falls Assessment]`, whereas the CPG requires screening when **no** falls risk assessment has been performed in the past year.; Consequently, **InPopulation** is also incorrectly set to `[Needs Falls Prevention Screening]`, leading to the recommendation being issued only for patients who already have a recent assessment, which is the opposite of the intended logic.

The ELM implementation defines **MeetsExclusionCriteria** as the conjunction (AND) of “Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)” and “Amiodarones”, whereas the CPG specifies that a patient should be excluded if **either** of these conditions is true (i.e., the logic should be OR). This mismatch in the exclusion logic violates the CPG decision criteria.

Value set reference mismatch – the ELM implementation uses the value set ID **2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564** for “Fall Risk Assessment,” whereas the CPG requires the value set ID **2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028**.

The ELM implementation references the value set **"Alcohol Use Screening"** with the identifier **2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278**, whereas the CPG specifies the value set **"Alcohol Use Screening"** with the identifier **2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542**. This mismatch in the value set ID means the screening check may not correctly identify prior alcohol misuse screenings as defined by the guideline.
